title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Colorado
description: Colorado will elect one U.S. senator in 2026 for the Class II seat currently held by Democrat John Hickenlooper. The race matters because it will help determine control of the U.S. Senate, and Colorado is generally viewed as a Democratic-leaning state in recent federal elections. The contest is likely to center on partisan control, candidate experience, and policy differences on issues such as the economy, health care, climate, and federal governance.
polling_note: No public general-election polling found for this race as of 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: John Hickenlooper
candidates.summary: John Hickenlooper is the incumbent U.S. senator from Colorado and a former governor and Denver mayor. He has emphasized a problem-solving, bipartisan style and has framed his Senate work around issues such as health care, climate, the economy, and public lands.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Colorado, 2026 - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: Geologist and businessman
career_history.description: Worked as a petroleum geologist and later co-founded one of Colorado's first brewpubs.

candidates.name: Karen Breslin
candidates.summary: Karen Breslin is listed by Ballotpedia as a candidate in the Democratic primary for Colorado's 2026 U.S. Senate race. Publicly available biographical information was limited in the sources reviewed, so her broader career history and education could not be confirmed from the materials found.

candidates.name: Julie Gonzales
candidates.summary: Julie Gonzales is listed by Ballotpedia as a candidate in the Democratic primary for Colorado's 2026 U.S. Senate race. She is known in Colorado politics as a state legislator and progressive organizer, and her campaign is likely to emphasize a more activist policy approach than the incumbent.
